redFlags.title: Not Computing MAT When Mandatory
redFlags.description: All companies (except those under 115BAA/115BAB) must compute MAT u/s 115JB and pay higher of normal tax or MAT. Skipping MAT computation is a critical error.
redFlags.consequence: Under-payment of tax. Interest u/s 234B and 234C. Scrutiny notice.
redFlags.correction: Always compute MAT on book profit. If MAT > normal tax, pay MAT and generate MATC credit.

redFlags.title: Applying Wrong Tax Rate (25% vs 30%)
redFlags.description: 25% rate applies only if the company's total turnover or gross receipts in the PREVIOUS financial year (FY 2024-25) did not exceed ₹400 Crore. Otherwise 30% applies.
redFlags.consequence: Under-payment of tax if 30% applied as 25%.
redFlags.correction: Verify prior-year turnover from last ITR-6 or audited accounts before selecting rate.

redFlags.title: Depreciation on Fully Depreciated Assets
redFlags.description: If WDV of a block has been reduced to zero in a prior year, no further depreciation can be claimed. Common error: continuing to claim depreciation on old assets.
redFlags.consequence: Incorrect depreciation reduces taxable income. May trigger scrutiny.
redFlags.correction: Check opening WDV block-by-block in Schedule DPM. If zero or negative (due to disposals), no depreciation.

redFlags.title: Excessive Related Party Payments Not Disallowed (Section 40A(2))
redFlags.description: Payments to directors, relatives, or associated companies above fair market value are disallowed u/s 40A(2). Transfer pricing rules (for international transactions) apply separately.
redFlags.consequence: Disallowance increases taxable income. Penalty for concealment.
redFlags.correction: Ensure all related party payments are at arm's length. Maintain documentation.

redFlags.title: Section 14A Disallowance Not Computed
redFlags.description: Expenses incurred to earn exempt income (dividends, MF returns) must be disallowed per Rule 8D method. Many companies skip this, understating taxable income.
redFlags.consequence: Disallowance increases taxable income. Interest and penalty if caught in assessment.
redFlags.correction: Compute Rule 8D disallowance: direct expenses + (total assets / total assets × interest + 1% of average value of investments) — see Rule 8D formula.

redFlags.title: CSR Expenditure Claimed as Business Deduction
redFlags.description: Section 37(1) Explanation 2: CSR expenditure u/s 135 of Companies Act 2013 is NOT deductible as business expense. Must be shown in Schedule CSR without deduction.
redFlags.consequence: If claimed as deduction, taxable income understated. Disallowance on assessment.
redFlags.correction: Show CSR spending in Schedule CSR. Do NOT include in business expenses.

redFlags.title: MATC Credit Not Utilized When Normal Tax Exceeds MAT
redFlags.description: In years when normal tax > MAT, the MATC credit from prior years (when MAT was paid) should be applied to reduce tax. Many companies forget this, over-paying tax.
redFlags.consequence: Excess tax payment. Cash flow loss.
redFlags.correction: Check Schedule MATC from prior returns. Apply available MATC credit in Part B-TTI.

redFlags.title: Insufficient Advance Tax — Companies Must Pay 100% by March 15
redFlags.description: Companies must pay advance tax in 4 installments: 15% by June 15, 45% by Sept 15, 75% by Dec 15, 100% by March 15. Shortfall in any installment attracts interest u/s 234C.
redFlags.consequence: Interest u/s 234C at 1% per month on each installment shortfall.
redFlags.correction: Estimate income early. Pay advance tax on time in correct proportions.

validationErrors.cause: Company did not compute MAT — mandatory for normal-rate companies
validationErrors.resolution: Fill Schedule MAT with book profit computation

validationErrors.errorMessage: Balance sheet totals do not match
validationErrors.resolution: Reconcile P&L, capital accounts, and balance sheet before filing

validationErrors.cause: All companies must file tax audit report — no exemption
validationErrors.resolution: Attach Form 3CA/3CD (company audit) or Form 3CB/3CD (non-company audit)
Get CIN from MCA21 portal. For foreign companies, provide registration number.
